MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-18-2019 IN 206, (DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE AUGUST 5, 2019 TRIAL DATE AND PRETRIAL DEADLINES. AMY SUPER, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 8/5/19 IS CONTINUED TO 1/13/20.) ****AT AROUND 9:57AM, THE MATTER WAS RECALLED DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BEING LATE AT THE INITIAL CALLING OF THE MOTION CALENDAR. DOW PATTEN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. AMY SUPER, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND VACATES THE TRIAL DATE OF 1/13/20 THAT WAS PREVIOUSLY SET. THE TRIAL DATE OF 8/5/19 IS CONTINUED TO 11/18/19 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL DEADLINES WILL BE GOVERNED BY THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR DEFENDANTS MOTION REQUESTING SANCTIONS. THE MOTION OF DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DORA BARNES HAS NOT FULLY COMPLIED WITH THE COURT'S PREVIOUS ORDER OF SEPTEMBER 18, 2018, WHICH GRANTED DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE VERIFICATION OF HER AUGUST 29, 2018 RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES BY DECEMBER 5, 2018. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE ALL PREVIOUSLY WITHHELD RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BY DECEMBER 5, 2018. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,387 BY DECEMBER 10, 2018. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: MELINDA DERISH, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amy Super, Esq. & Kate G. Kimberlin, Esq., Deputy City Attorneys, T:415.554.3847 for defendant/moving party;
Dow W. Patten, Esq. of Smith Patten, T:415.402.0084, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested & argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish her claims for race and gender discrimination and retaliation.

[See word minutes for the Court's full ruling.]

Order and Judgment were signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2018 Set For Trial On Aug-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Defendants Motion Requesting Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Defendants Motion Requesting Sanctions.

The motion of defendant City and County of San Francisco is granted. Plaintiff Dora Barnes has not fully complied with the court's previous order of September 18, 2018, which granted defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's response to defendant's form interrogatories and requests for production of documents. Plaintiff shall provide verification of her August 29, 2018 responses to defendant's form interrogatories by December 5, 2018. Plaintiff shall provide all previously withheld responsive documents by December 5, 2018. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,387 by December 10, 2018.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Response To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kate G. Kimberlin, Deputy City Attorney CCSF, Telephone (415) 554-3847, appearing for Defendants City and County of San Francisco, et al;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Response To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents.

Motion granted. Plaintiff shall provide within 10 days complete, objection-free, verified responses (and all responsive documents) to defendant City and County of San Francisco's Requests for Production and Form Interrogatories. The Court notes that CCSF in its reply brief withdrew the motion as to RFAs and Special Interrogatories.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Chuck Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
